text: The cadence thing I think is a big part of why Harden has struggled in the playoffs over his career. So much of his game is about stringing together moves that look the same - several jab steps or slow crossovers to lull you to sleep before the one where he suddenly drives to the rim or launches a step-back. That stuff is obviously really effective against the minimal level of preparation an NBA team puts into a typical regular season game. But I think defenders who are locked in for the playoffs, who have been studying film and played against him for five games in a row, can start to unlock his patterns and play a step ahead of him.
